explicit_text: Fort Leonard Wood, Missouri, was where I went for Basic Training starting January 1968. It was a place with about 45,000 to 60,000 troops at the time, and I remember our Drill Instructor, Sergeant Bernard Heistan, who was a tough character. We stayed in new back barracks with eight guys per room, and it was my first real experience away from home and the start of Army discipline.
